perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) A woman sits in front of a camera holding a piece of fried chicken dipped in a white sauce. She takes a bite and smiles at the camera. (2) Chronological cuts show her eating various parts of the chicken piece, focusing on removing all meat from the bone. Her facial expressions convey enjoyment. (3) The final sequence shows her holding up a completely clean chicken bone, then picking up another piece of chicken to repeat the process.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) An upbeat Korean rap/pop song begins with a steady rhythm and both male and female vocals. No spoken narration is detected. (2) The music continues at a consistent volume throughout the eating demonstration. (3) Subtle chewing and crunching sounds are occasionally audible beneath the music track as she bites into the chicken.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) 'POV 有潔癖的韓國人怎麼吃炸雞？' (How a germaphobe/tidy Korean eats fried chicken?) - Hook. (2) '1. 上面先啃一圈' (1. First chew around the top) and '好好吃😋' (So delicious) - Instructional/Emotional. (3) '2. 中段的肉也要咬一咬，不能剩' (2. The meat in the middle must also be bitten, nothing left) and '3. 最後把底部那一塊 (一口吸出來)' (3. Finally the bottom piece (suck it out in one go)) - Functional instructions. (4) '無論是雞腿還是雞翅，請吃到只剩骨頭!!!' (Whether it's a drumstick or a wing, please eat until only the bone is left!!!) and '-以上是韓國人的吃雞名言-' (-Above is the famous saying of Koreans eating chicken-) - Concluding emphasis.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 female main creator in center frame
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: Young woman with long, straight brown hair, wearing a black t-shirt, appearing in a home setting.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: A domestic kitchen/dining area with light wood-style cabinets and a countertop visible behind the woman.

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: List-based: Hook -> Action Step 1 -> Action Step 2 -> Action Step 3 -> Summary Result
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video is a humorous instructional guide on how to eat fried chicken 'properly' according to a perceived cultural standard of tidiness in Korea. It aims to entertain viewers with a mukbang-style demonstration while showcasing the 'perfect' way to leave bones clean.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: No specific brand name or logo is identified for the food or the sauce.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: Use of POV hook and step-by-step text overlays
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video leverages cultural stereotypes about Korean dining habits to create relatable or aspirational 'clean eating' content for a food-loving audience.
